opinion.h: KELLEY ET AL. v. RAGGIO ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF NEVADA. No. 316. Decided November 7, 1960.
opinion.p: Appeal dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
Roger D. Foley, Attorney General of Nevada, John A. Porter and Norman H. Samuelson, Deputy Attorneys General, and William J. Raggio for appellees.
The appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
opinion.h: CENTRAL ILLINOIS PUB. SER. v. ILL. COMMERCE COMM'N, 364 U.S. 336 (1960)
opinion.p: 364 U.S. 336 CENTRAL ILLINOIS PUBLIC SERVICE CO. v. ILLINOIS COMMERCE COMM'N ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF ILLINOIS. No. 352. Decided November 7, 1960.
William L. Guild, Attorney General of Illinois, Harry R. Begley, Special Assistant Attorney General, John W. Foster, Robert Mitten, Joseph H. Wright and Herbert J. Deany for appellees.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed. Treating the papers whereon the appeal was taken as a petition for writ of certiorari, certiorari is denied.
